name: Issue voucher by member ID
description: Documented success response (Sample JSON Response, lines 23099-23114). status=true with empty results because the inner result fields are org-configured for the IssueVoucher process. requestExample is the documented member-ID request (lines 23066-23084).
notes: Resource is the generic Loyalty Program Process API (Available version 56.0, HTTP method POST; lines 22598-22603); processName=IssueVoucher. Request wrapper property is `processParameters` typed as a Loyalty Process Parameter Map (Required, 56.0; lines 22626-22629). Response body schema is named "Loyalty Process Output" (line 22633); it is NOT broken out as a separate Response Bodies entry, so the authoritative shape is the inline Sample JSON Response (message/outputParameters/simulationDetails/status). IMPLEMENTER-DEFINED: the doc explicitly states "The request and response parameters vary depending on the input and output parameters configured for the IssueVoucher process type in a Salesforce org" (lines 23059-23060). Therefore the inner fields of each `processParameters` object (MemberId, MembershipNumber, VoucherCode, VoucherFaceValue, VoucherDiscountPercentage, VoucherExpirationDate) and the contents of `outputParameters.outputParameters.results[]` (shown as `{}` / empty in the doc) are org-configured, treat their presence/names as configuration, not as a fixed contract. Some request field names are interchangeable (see "Loyalty Management Connect API Interchangeable Request Fields", note at lines 23064-23065). Field names/casing copied verbatim from the doc examples. No error/empty fixture provided, the doc shows no error-case JSON for this process. Available version 56.0 is the resource version; the example URL in the doc uses v67.0 (Summer 26).
